summary: Extract a structured implementation spec from the Paragon Pioneers 2 research doc

tiers.name: Pioneers
tiers.upgrade_threshold_note: Upgrades to Colonist's House only when ALL basic needs AND luxuries hit 100% simultaneously. Occupancy itself driven by basic-need fulfilment: a Pioneer's Hut starts 2/10, reaches 10/10 once a Well supplies Water.

tiers.name: Colonists
tiers.upgrade_threshold_note: Upgrades to Townsmen's House only when ALL needs AND luxuries hit 100% simultaneously. Colonist basic needs == Pioneer basics + Pioneer luxuries (Fish/Sausage/Cider/Water/Community).

tiers.name: Townsmen
tiers.upgrade_threshold_note: Upgrades to Merchant's Mansion only when ALL needs AND luxuries hit 100% simultaneously. Townsmen basics == Colonist luxuries (Soap/Fabric/Bread/Education) plus carried-up Water/Community.

tiers.name: Merchants

luxury_needs.raw: Hat 1/8000 (TERMINAL — does NOT carry up to Paragon basics; the one cascade exception)

tiers.upgrade_threshold_note: Upgrades to Paragon's Residence only when ALL needs AND luxuries hit 100% simultaneously. Merchant basics == Townsmen luxuries (Cauldron/Beer/Leather/Coffee/Jam) plus carried-up Water/Community/Education/Medical Care.

tiers.name: Paragons
tiers.upgrade_threshold_note: Top of urban chain (no further tier). Paragon basics == Merchant luxuries EXCEPT Hat (terminal), so Pastry/Tobacco Pipe/Caviar/Gold Jewelry/Perfume plus carried-up Community/Education/Medical Care/Administration/Hygiene.

tiers.name: Farmers

basic_needs.raw: Fish 1/3300 (note: rural Fish rate differs from urban 3780)

tiers.upgrade_threshold_note: Rural chain. Upgrades to Worker's House only when ALL needs AND luxuries hit 100% simultaneously.

tiers.name: Workers

luxury_needs.raw: Beer 1/16400 (note: Worker Beer 16400 differs from Townsmen Beer 17900)

tiers.upgrade_threshold_note: Top of rural chain (no further tier). Worker basics == Farmer basics + Farmer luxuries (Fish/Tea/Rum/Cheese/Cigar/Water/Tropical Community/Sports).

pop.cascade_explanation: THE LUXURY CASCADE (the heart of the game, and literal): each tier's LUXURY needs are exactly the BASIC needs of the tier directly above it — SAME good name AND SAME per-resident rate. Consumption is always '1 unit per N seconds' per resident. Two chains follow this rule: (1) the 5-tier URBAN chain Pioneers -> Colonists -> Townsmen -> Merchants -> Paragons; (2) the 2-tier RURAL chain Farmers -> Workers. Verified by the verbatim rates: Pioneer luxuries Sausage 1/10800 + Cider 1/9450 reappear as Colonist basics at the identical 10800 / 9450; Colonist luxuries Soap 1/12600, Fabric 1/20160, Bread 1/16800 reappear as Townsmen basics; Townsmen luxuries Cauldron 1/45400, Beer 1/17900, Leather 1/22100, Coffee 1/20900, Jam 1/13400 reappear as Merchant basics; Merchant luxuries Pastry 1/8000, Tobacco Pipe 1/9000, Caviar 1/36000, Gold Jewelry 1/12400, Perfume 1/12400 reappear as Paragon basics. Service needs (Water, Community, Education, Medical Care, Administration, Hygiene) also carry upward. THE ONE EXCEPTION: Hat (Merchant luxury, 1/8000) is a TERMINAL good — it does NOT carry up into Paragon basics. The cascade also implies an ordering constraint for the clone: do not consume a tier's luxury good before its luxury production chain exists, because upgrading a house before building that tier's luxury chain temporarily kills its output. ASCENSION/UPGRADE GATE: a house levels up IN PLACE (Pioneer's Hut -> Colonist's House, etc.) only when ALL basic needs AND ALL luxuries hit 100% SIMULTANEOUSLY. Occupancy (resident count up to cap) is driven separately by BASIC-need fulfilment (Pioneer's Hut starts 2/10, reaches 10/10 once Water is supplied), while LUXURY fulfilment gates the tier's productive OUTPUT. Unmet needs cause EMIGRATION only — never death or revolt; residents leave and return. Documented exploit (confirmed in PP2, wording from PP1): hoard luxuries on offshore ships so they aren't consumed, dump them to spike a house to 100% to trigger the permanent tier unlock, then downgrade back to cheap huts to keep earning.
pop.coin_gold_output_note: Output TYPE by tier: Pioneers -> Militia (+Coins); Farmers -> Slingers (+Coins); Paragons -> Favor; everyone else (Colonists, Townsmen, Merchants, Workers) -> Coins ('Gold'). CRITICAL: the per-resident Coin/Gold OUTPUT rate is UNDOCUMENTED anywhere — only CONSUMPTION rates are published. The doc instructs modeling income as: income = base_tier_rate × luxury_satisfaction_fraction (i.e. output scales linearly with the fraction of luxury needs met). So a clone must pick its own base_tier_rate values; only the consumption denominators above are authoritative game data.
pop.notes: Source: C:/Users/grima/Documents/aiDev/docs/paragon-pioneers-2-clone-research.html, lines 388-550 (sections #cascade, #tiers, #needs). RATE CONFIDENCE: the verbatim consumption table (lines 484-524) is tagged 'verbatim from wiki · high confidence'. Rate discrepancies to preserve in the clone: urban Fish = 1/3780 but rural (Farmers/Workers) Fish = 1/3300; Townsmen Beer = 1/17900 but Worker Beer = 1/16400 (Beer appears in both chains at different rates). Goods vs Services: 'Goods' consumed continuously at 1/N sec per resident; 'Services' are binary coverage (a provider building exists & is supplied) — but data-mining shows services are actually implemented as abstract consumable goods (Water, Community, Education...) routed through the warehouse network, NOT a tile radius. Service buildings & costs (lines 529-545, 14 services): Water=Well (10 Wood) / Drywell (tropical) / Cistern (research) [all tiers]; Community=Tavern (300 + 20 Plank) / Harbor Tavern [Pioneers->Merchants]; Education=School (210 + 100 Plank) / Town School [Colonists->Paragons]; Medical Care=Medicus (300 + 90 Coin + 100 Limestone + 50 Mortar) [Townsmen->Paragons]; Hygiene=Bathhouse (400 + 300 Coin + 150 Brick + 100 Mortar + 75 Tool) [Merchants, Paragons]; Administration=Kontor I-V / Town Hall / Senate (scales by tier) [Merchants, Paragons]; Coiffeur=Coiffeur (1 Paragon + 900 Coin + 100 Marble + 75 Tool) [Paragons]; Higher Education=University (700 + 900 Coin + 100 Marble + 75 Tool + 100 Window) [Paragons]; Entertainment=Theatre (900 + 900 Coin + 100 Marble + 75 Tool + 100 Window) / Fair [Paragons, Workers]; Gambling=Fair/casino-type (cost undocumented) [Paragons]; Tropical Community / Sports = tropical Community / Sports Ground (400 + 90 Coin + 100 Fiber) [Farmers, Workers]; Trade Opportunities / Cemetery = Market Hall (50 + 120 Coin + 100 Fiber + 150 Brick) / Cemetery [Workers]. DLC MAGIC TRACK (separate from the 7 tiers above; capacities UNDOCUMENTED): Apprentices (Apprentice Dormitory, 20 Wood) -> Adepts (Adepts' Quarter, 20 Plank + 40 Basalt) -> Archmages (Archmages' Tower, 60 Basalt + 60 Marble); Apprentices & Adepts produce Mana, Archmages produce Elder Mana. PARTIAL/AGGREGATED RATES: the verbatim table lists Colonist/Merchant/Paragon/Worker carried-up basics as '(as above)' or shows them inline (e.g. Colonist basic 'Fish / Sausage / Cider = 3780 / 10800 / 9450') rather than re-stating each — I expanded them per the cascade rule into the per-tier need arrays. Cider luxury for Pioneers shown in diagram as 'Cider 1/9450'.

recipes.notes: Group: raw. Harvests an adjacent terrain tile (no input good).
Group: forestry. Cycle listed as em-dash (unknown). Input is a forest tile, not a stored good.

recipes.notes: Group: processing. Cycle listed as em-dash (unknown). Sawmill (river variant) doubles output. The river marker applies to the Sawmill variant.
Group: food. Output +25% per adjacent Waterfield.
Group: food (research). Researched upgrade of Piggery.
Group: food. (Distinct from raw Wheat Field which is 1 Wheat/960s.)

Group: processing. Recipe table reads '1 Coal + 2 Iron -> 2 Iron Ingot'; the '2 Iron' input is Iron Ore (diagram says '1 Coal + 2 Iron -> 2 Ingot /960s').
Group: processing. '1 Zinc' input is Zinc Ore.
Group: processing. '1 Lead' input is Lead Ore.
Group: luxury. Cross-region: Ambergris from Northern whales, Brandy from Merchant tier.
Group: food. Recipe reads '-> 1 Grapes (12 draws)'.
Group: food (N). Recipe reads '2 River -> 1 Sturgeon' (consumes 2 river tiles/spots).
Group: tropical. Recipe reads '4 -> 1 Milk' (input good unlabeled in source; presumed Grass).

Source: C:/Users/grima/Documents/aiDev/docs/paragon-pioneers-2-clone-research.html, lines 551-733 (Goods & Production chains section). All recipe numbers read verbatim from the "Complete recipe reference" table (data-mined from the ElQDuck calculator: CONSUME_PER_ITERATION / PRODUCE_PER_ITERATION / ITERATION_TIME_IN_SECONDS). Goods counts: store says "130+"; QDuck calculator models 148 tradeable goods; wiki Goods page lists ~166 distinct physical goods + 6 Global currencies. Buildings count is separate (~226-300; QDuck ~246 building files, Steam markets "300+"). KEY DESIGN RULE: every building has exactly ONE recipe and ONE scalar output (PRODUCE_PER_ITERATION is a scalar) - NO byproducts, no building emits two goods. No worker assignment. Chains emerge by joining one building's output to another's input. ★ goods are added/used by the Isle of Magicians DLC; Magic-tier (DLC) recipe ratios are NOT data-mined and remain unknown (excluded above). RIVER GATING: '⌁river' = requires a straight river tile (RiverField input) — applies to Sawmill (river variant of Saw Works), Flour Mill, Tannery, Steel Furnace, Powder Mill, Cannon Foundry, Musket Maker, Paper Mill, Brocade Spinning Factory, Winepress, Sugar Mill. NAME RECONCILIATION: 'Meat Pie' (Goods page) = 'Pastry' (Population page) — same canonical good. AMBIGUITIES in source flagged in per-recipe notes: (1) Iron Smelter recipe printed as '1 Coal + 2 Iron -> 2 Iron Ingot' where '2 Iron' = Iron Ore (confirmed by diagram '1 Coal + 2 Iron -> 2 Ingot'); Zinc/Lead smelters similarly use ore inputs labeled just 'Zinc'/'Lead'. (2) Hop Farm printed '8 Hop -> 1 Hops' (diagram simplifies to 'Hop Farm -> Hops /120s'). (3) Goat Farm printed '4 -> 1 Milk' with input good unlabeled (presumed Grass). (4) Glassworks '5 Quartz' = Quartz Sand. (5) Ornate Tailor 'Fur' input likely = Pelt. (6) Sturgeon Farm '2 River -> 1 Sturgeon' consumes river spots. (7) Lumberjack and Saw Works/Sawmill cycle times printed as em-dash (unknown). (8) Winery output reads '1 Grapes (12 draws)'. The 11 Service buildings at line 731 are modelled as recipes too (fuel-consuming); for free services (Well/Cistern/Tavern/School) only a per-something quantity is printed without a separate iteration time except Well (20/1s) — iteration_time_seconds left null where the source did not separate it. Diagram-only late-chain note: late chains run 4-6 steps with multi-branch convergence and cross-region dependency (e.g. Perfume needs Northern Ambergris + Merchant Brandy + Rose Water).

buildings.notes:
Source: C:/Users/grima/Documents/aiDev/docs/paragon-pioneers-2-clone-research.html, lines 735-1141 (the "Buildings catalog" section). All values are verbatim from the wiki-derived tables.

FOOTPRINT: Tile footprints are undocumented for EVERY building per the intro text (line 737): "Tile footprints are undocumented everywhere - expect mostly 1x1-3x3, to be measured later." I set footprint to "undocumented (expect 1x1-3x3)" for all rows; the exact text appears once and applies catalog-wide.

BUILD COST FORMAT: I combined the doc's two cost columns - "Goods cost" + "Coins" - into one build_cost string as "<goods>, <N> Coins". A "-" in goods means no material cost; "0 Coins" / "- Coins" reproduces the doc's literal coin value (Northern region uses "-" for several coins cells).

TIER_UNLOCK FORMAT: I packed two facts into this field: (1) the population line + temperate/tropical region tier from the <summary>, and (2) the per-row "Unlock" cell which is the actual gate - either a population-count threshold (e.g. "70 Colonists", "300 Townsmen"), "1 <Pop>" (available immediately on reaching that tier), "Research", "-" (starter/base), or "Custodian" (special). The population threshold IS the only workforce gate; there is no assigned-worker number.

CATEGORY maps to BuildingConfig.kind per the doc. Categories used verbatim: Raw/forestry, Raw/fisher, Raw/animal, Raw/field, Raw/mine, Raw/quarry, Raw/hunter, Raw (generic), Processing, Food, Storage, Residential, Military, Military (weapon), Military (training), Military/magic (training), Service (+ subtypes: water, education, doctor, hygiene, warmth, magic edu, administration, entertainment, higher edu), Service/trade, Service/research, Service/culture, Food/service, Infrastructure, Infrastructure/harbor, Infrastructure (portal), Monument, Decoration.

NAME DISAMBIGUATION: Several building names repeat across tiers/regions with different costs (Kontor I-V, Warehouse I-V, Garrison I-V, Copper Mine, Copper Smelter, Rock Salt Mine, Salt Works, Sawmill, Ropery, Shipyard, Labor Camp, Cargo Kontor, Ship Guild, Linseed Field/Farm, Brass Smelter, Lumberjack, Forester, Pantheon, Palace Foundation, Saltern, Fisherman's Hut, Gold Mine, Gold Smelter, Coal Mine, Rickyard). Where the same name appears in multiple tables I appended a parenthetical qualifier (e.g. "(tropical)", "(Northern)", "(DLC)", "(T4 re-list)") so each row is unique; the base name from the wiki is the part before the parenthesis. The Merchants tier deliberately RE-LISTS several earlier raws (Copper Mine, Copper Smelter, Rock Salt Mine, Salt Works) at a cheaper Wood cost but higher Coin cost ("cheap re-list") - both the original and the re-list are preserved.

TERRAIN REQUIREMENTS captured verbatim where given: "River required" (Sawmill, Flour Mill, Mortar Batch Works, Textile Factory, Tannery, Feltmaking Mill [listed as "River"], Linen Weaver, Sawmill DLC); "Ocean" / "Ocean-side" (all Kontors, Shipyards, Cargo Kontors, Ship Guilds, Harbor Tavern); "Not on mountain top" (Stonecutter, Marble Quarry, Basaltcutter); "Water next to beach" (Weir, Salmon Farm); "Beach" (Beach Fisherman's Hut). Most buildings list no terrain requirement ("-"). Advanced Shipyard's ocean requirement is implied by ship-building but not stated in its row.

NOTABLE EXACT NUMBERS / THRESHOLDS:
- Population thresholds per tier scale: Pioneers 10/20/80/120/300; Colonists 70/140/210/280/350/400; Townsmen 100/200/300/400/500; Merchants 100/200/300/400/500/600/700; Paragons 100/200/300/400/500/600/700/800/900/1,000; Farmers 50/100/150/200/300/400; Workers 50/100/150/200/250/300/400/450/500; Magic tiers 50/100/150/200/250/300/400/500.
- Residence capacities: Pioneer's Hut 10 (starts 2/10); Colonist's House ~15; Townsmen's House 20; Merchant's Mansion 25; Paragon's Residence 30; Farmer's Shack ~10; Worker's House ~20.
- Monuments are the largest sinks: Palace Foundation (temperate) 3,000 Planks + 2,000 Tools + 10,000 Coins with 5 upgrade stages via Favor; Palace Foundation (Worker) 1,000 Fiber + 500 Brick + 1,000 Fine Wood + 10,000 Coins; Pantheon 200 Marble + 100-150 Tools + 1,650 Coins (Worker variant 100 Fine Wood + 200 Marble + 1,650 Coins).
- Marble Quarry has a range on both unlock (600-700 Merchants) and cost (20 Wood OR 90 Brick+60 Mortar+45 Tool; 150-200 Coins) - ambiguity preserved verbatim.
- Portal III requires the special resource "80 Elder Mana" in addition to Basalt+Marble.

CATALOG TOTALS per the <summary> pins: Pioneers 21, Colonists 35, Townsmen 36, Merchants 48, Paragons 50, Farmers 45, Workers 47, Northern 12, Apprentices 20, Adepts 16, Archmages 17. I captured every row in all 11 tables. (Some pin counts are off by one or two versus actual visible rows - e.g. Pioneers table shows 20 data rows vs pin "21", Workers shows 47 - this matches the source as written; I did not invent rows to reconcile.)

The intro also notes: "Coins is the literal coin cost (a real construction sink alongside materials)" and star tiers (Apprentices/Adepts/Archmages) require the Isle of Magicians DLC. There is no per-building assigned-worker count anywhere - the population threshold is the only workforce gate.

terrain_types.name: Deep ocean / coast
terrain_types.constraint: "Must be built next to the ocean"
terrain_types.example_buildings: Kontor, shipyards, Cargo Kontor, Harbor Tavern

terrain_types.name: Beach / shallow water
terrain_types.constraint: "Must be built in the water next to the beach"

terrain_types.name: River ("straight river spot")
terrain_types.constraint: "Must be built on a straight river spot"
terrain_types.example_buildings: Sawmill, Flour Mill, Tannery, Textile/Steel/Powder/Cannon/Paper mills

terrain_types.name: Mountain
terrain_types.constraint: Most ore/stone deposits; Pegasus Farm "on top of a mountain" (DLC)
terrain_types.example_buildings: Mines, quarries; research can move marble/coal off mountains

terrain_types.name: Forest (tree density 0-3+)
terrain_types.constraint: Need forest tiles; Arboretum replants tiles with <3 trees

regions.name: Temperate (start)
regions.signature_goods: Wheat/bread, hops/beer, apples/cider, copper/iron/gold/zinc/lead, marble, wool/fabric, horses

regions.name: Tropical
regions.signature_goods: Tea, sugar→rum/sugar, coffee, cacao→chocolate, tobacco→cigars, silk, indigo, cotton, mahogany, gems

regions.name: Northern
regions.signature_goods: Conifer wood, salmon, coal, ores, gems, whale→ambergris; "Heating Plant" replaces the Water need

regions.name: High Seas (DLC)
regions.signature_goods: Mana/Elder Mana, basalt, potatoes→schnapps, linen/cloak, pegasus; magic academies
regions.access: Isle of Magicians DLC; "faster to reach"

world.cartography_island_gen: Procedural islands via "Cartography": Islands are generated by a parameterized map generator. When you order a ship to discover one, you spend Cartography points to set its Size (~12→large), number of mountains, rivers, fertilities (which crops grow, cap 4) and deposits (ores, cap 4). Each Cartography point spent adds +10 min to the discovery time. Clay & saltpeter are mountain-free "bonus deposits". Research moves things off mountains (Marble Pit, Coal Pit), enlarges islands (Bigger Islands +2/level), or clears trees.
world.expansion_loop: Repeatable three-step loop, capped by a per-run island limit (raised by region-unlock research, "Better Relations" +1, and the Minion custodian +2). Step 1 · Discover: Send ship(s) to scout; the slowest ship sets duration. Key numbers: travel time + 10 min per Cartography point; "Better Spyglasses" −35%; total cap 2.5 days. Step 2 · Conquer: Clear Orc camps via auto-battle, or bribe them. Cleared land becomes buildable. Key numbers: win if all enemies die (even if all yours do); you can attrition — Orcs never reinforce. Step 3 · Keep / hand over: Keep & settle, hand the island to the Paragons for Favor, or turn it in for Coins. Defeating a warchief grants a Cartography point.
world.favor_formula: Favor = Max(0,(Size−26)×16) + 2^((Min(Size,26)−10)/2 − 1) — doubles per size to 26, then +32/size
world.logistics_range_warehouse: Logistics between islands: Simple (2-island) and Complex (multi-waypoint) Trade Routes move goods AND military units — but NEVER population. A Cargo Kontor halves the offshore "cargo queue". Ships are region-locked or region-crossing. DLC Portals give instant inter-island transfer. Islands are linked only by ship trade routes (and, with the DLC, instant portals) — never by land. Note: the read range (lines 298-386) does not document an on-island tile-range / proximity connectivity warehouse mechanic ("no roads"); the World section frames inter-island logistics as ship-route-based, with the micro-level being each island's own top-down square-grid build map using whole-tile footprints (no isometric, no rotation).
world.notes: Source: C:/Users/grima/Documents/aiDev/docs/paragon-pioneers-2-clone-research.html, lines 298-386 (World/terrain/expansion panel). Two-level world: macro = small set of separate climate regions (open sea dotted with discrete islands reached by ship); micro = each island's own top-down square-grid build map. Run many simultaneous settlements across regions, linked only by ship (never by land). Climate count: 3 base + 1 DLC. Climate hard-gates BOTH production and population, forcing a multi-island supply network (e.g. a temperate Merchant city must ship in tropical coffee & chocolate). Rivers + water-mills are a headline new feature vs PP1. Build grid uses whole-tile footprints; no isometric, no rotation. IMPORTANT: The requested 'range/warehouse proximity connectivity (no roads)' detail is NOT present verbatim in lines 298-386 — that on-island logistics mechanic likely lives in a different section of the doc (e.g. buildings/logistics). Flagged in logistics_range_warehouse field. All captured numbers verbatim: Tropical research 25 Creativity (Townsmen tree); Northern research 175 Creativity (Merchants tree); Cartography Size ~12→large; fertilities cap 4; deposits cap 4; +10 min per Cartography point; Bigger Islands +2/level; Better Spyglasses −35%; discovery total cap 2.5 days; island limit raisers: Better Relations +1, Minion custodian +2; Favor formula captured verbatim with code markup."

currencies.name: Coin
currencies.earned_by: Population 'Gold' — Colonists/Townsmen/Merchants/Workers produce Coins at 100% luxuries (per-resident rate unpublished); turning an island in for Coins; custodians (Treasurer +10,000, Enchanter 4x handover coins); minting at Coining Press (1 Gold Ingot -> 7 Coin / 240s).
currencies.spent_on: Literal coin construction cost on nearly every building (a real sink alongside materials); Kontor upgrades; the Worker-line Palace is fueled by Coins. No upkeep, wages, or bankruptcy — Coin is a non-negative global wallet you spend down.

currencies.name: Cartography
currencies.earned_by: Defeating a warchief grants 1 Cartography point. Custodians can grant a start (Cartographer = start with 30 cartography; Enchanter 2x cartography).
currencies.spent_on: Discovering procedural islands — discovery duration = travel time + 10 min per Cartography point ('Better Spyglasses' -35%; total cap 2.5 days).

currencies.name: Favor
currencies.earned_by: Hand an island over to the Paragons. Favor = Max(0,(Size-26)*16) + 2^((Min(Size,26)-10)/2 - 1) (doubles per size up to 26, then +32/size). Top-tier Paragon residents (Paragon's Residence) produce Favor.
currencies.spent_on: Fuels the (Paragon-line) Palace and its 5 upgrade stages — the prestige path.

currencies.name: Reputation
currencies.earned_by: Each completed Palace grants one permanent, NON-CONSUMABLE Reputation point. Bonus Reputation from taking one of the 12 Challenges.
currencies.spent_on: Custodians — run-modifiers re-pickable each restart (Custodians cost Rep tiers 1/2/3/4/8). Reputation is permanent, not spent down.

currencies.name: Mana
currencies.earned_by: DLC (Isle of Magicians / High Seas) magic track: Apprentices and Adepts produce Mana.

currencies.name: Elder Mana
currencies.earned_by: DLC magic track: Archmages (Archmages' Tower) produce Elder Mana.
currencies.spent_on: Top-tier magic buildings, e.g. Portal III (In/Out) cost 80 Elder Mana each (+200 Basalt + 200 Marble).

systems.combat:
AUTO-RESOLVED, OFFENSIVE-ONLY. Only enemy = Orcs in static forts on every island; on default difficulty Orcs never attack you (no walls/towers/garrison-defense/patrols, no naval combat). Pick troops to send (army CAPPED AT 100 units; raised by Bannerman +50, General x2), battle auto-resolves over real idle wall-clock time, notification on end, no tactical control. Clearing a fort opens buildable land; defeating a warchief grants 1 Cartography point.

RESOLUTION MODEL: Rounds until one side is wiped. Each round has 3 phases: First strike -> Normal -> Last strike (abilities set phase membership; Double Strike acts in both first & last; Triple Strike acts in all three; no strike keyword = normal phase 2 only). All attackers in a phase hit SIMULTANEOUSLY; a unit killed earlier deals no later damage. Crit DOUBLES damage; crit chance = 0.8 player / 0.6 normal Orc / 0.5 boss. WIN IF ALL ENEMIES DIE — even if all your units also die. On a LOSS, your kills stay dead but injured enemies fully heal (attrition works; Orcs never reinforce).

DURATION FORMULA: time = (Sum of unit.tier x count over BOTH armies) x lerp(18, 28, tiers/1000) seconds, capped at 12h. Berserk = (t-2h)/2. Encouraging (War Drummer) = -1 minute. Unit tiers: Pioneer units 1-2, Colonist 2, Townsmen/Farmers 3, Merchants/Workers 4, Paragons 5, bosses 100-600 (tier is a DURATION weight, NOT HP), supporters 0.

PLAYER UNITS (HP/Atk/Tier/abilities): Militia (Pioneers) 15/5/1/—; Archer (Colonists) 10/20/1/Ranged; Footsoldier (Colonists) 40/15/1/—; Longbow Archer (Townsmen) 10/15/2/Double,Ranged; Cavalry (Townsmen) 5/5/2/First,Flank; Knight (Merchants) 90/20/3/—; Crossbowman (Merchants) 15/90/3/Ranged; Cuirassier (Paragons) 120/10/4/First; Cannoneer (Paragons) 60/80/4/Last,Ranged,Flank,Trample.

12 WARCHIEF BOSSES (one per island size 12->34 step 2; HP not monotonic; Mazoga tankiest) — Size/HP/Dmg/abilities: Bula 12/2,500/150/Ranged,Last,Splash; Durz 14/1,100/300/Ranged,First,Splash; Hork 16/12,000/100/Ranged,Last,Splash,Flank; Aguk 18/7,000/250/Last,Splash; Kul'Tan 20/10,000/500/Armageddon,Ranged,Triple; Mazoga 22/120,000/100/Last,Splash; Durgash 24/40,000/500/Ranged,First,Splash; Zrall 26/45,000/100/Lightning Bolt,First,Splash; Krashek 28/35,000/1,500/Confusion,First,Splash; Selzok 30/10,000/250/Summon,Ranged,Last,Splash; Saukron 32/15,000/3,000/Bulletproof,Last,Splash,Spiky; Nur'Zhel 34/75,000/1,250/Revive,Ranged,First,Splash.

~28 ABILITY KEYWORDS (resolver rule set): First/Last/Double/Triple Strike (phase membership); Ranged (only attackable once all melee on its side dead); Flanking (targets lowest-max-HP enemy first; Ranged does NOT protect); Trample/Splash (overkill carries to next target); Charge (hits all enemy groups, stopped by Defender); Defender (blocks Charge); Piercing (hits all groups); Armageddon (5 dmg to EVERY unit both sides each round); Lightning Bolt (each round kills enemy unit-type with highest total HP); Confusion (forces enemies to last-strike phase only); Summon (boss spawns 100 orklings + 400 orcslings/round); Revive (resurrect half of fallen allies each round, all at battle end); Stabilization (50% save dying ally); Necromancer custodian (50% chance each fallen PLAYER unit resurrects after battle); Explosive (kills the melee unit that killed it); Spiky (melee attacker takes 5 dmg back, e.g. Pikemen reflect); Bulletproof (immune to ranged, stops ranged Piercing); Shielded (1/3 ranged dmg); Stalwart (-45 dmg taken min 2, doubled vs crits); Quicksilver (only 2 dmg from non-splash); Encouraging (-1 min duration); Pillage (+1% loot); Supporter (no attack, doesn't count to 100-unit limit); Flying / Chaotic Spellcasting (DLC mage; Chaotic = 50% chance for 3 unblockable AoE hits).

RECRUITING: Population produces a base unit when luxuries met (Pioneers->Militia, Farmers->Slingers); training buildings consume base unit + weapon good. Units are goods with NO upkeep. Training recipes (recipe / cycle / tier): Boot Camp 1 Militia + 1 Plank -> 1 Squire /5m (Pioneers research); Barracks 2 Militia + 1 Copper Sword -> 1 Footsoldier /10m (Colonists); Archery Range 1 Militia + 1 Bow -> 1 Archer /5m (Colonists); Bowyer 3 Wood + 2 Fiber -> 1 Bow /4m (weapon); Knight Barracks 4 Militia + 1 Iron Sword -> 1 Knight /20m (Merchants); Poleturner's Workshop 1 Coal + 1 Copper Ingot -> 1 Pike /8m; Pikemen Barracks 3 Slinger + 1 Pike -> 1 Pikeman /15m (Farmers); Glaive Smith 1 Fine Wood + 2 Gemstone + 1 Copper Ingot -> 1 Glaive /8m; Cannon Foundry (river) 1 Gunpowder + 1 Steel -> 1 Cannon /8m; Cannoneer's School 6 Militia + 1 Cannon -> 1 Cannoneer /30m (Paragons). Garrison capacity per island raised by Quartermaster x2 / Mercenary x3 / Garrison Expansion research.
systems.ships:
Ships built at 4 shipyard tiers (2 ships each), move goods/units (NEVER population) on Simple or Complex Trade Routes. Capacity = slots x slot-size; bracketed slot-size = researched upgrade. Region-locked starters vs region-crossing hulls is the central naval gate.

ROSTER (Ship | Region | Slots×size | Speed | Goods/h | Coin | Cross region?):
- Cog | Temperate | 2×60(80) | 100%-2h | 60 | 100 | No
- Caravel | Temperate | 2×40 | 200%-1h | 80 | 150 | No
- Hulk | Temperate | 4×90(120) | 100%-2h | 180 | 500 | Yes
- Pinnace | Temperate | 3×60 | 300%-40m | 270 | 750 | Yes
- Galleon | Temperate | 6×180(240) | 100%-2h | 540 | 4,000 | Yes
- Clipper | Temperate | 4×100 | 400%-30m | 800 | 6,000 | Yes
- Schooner | Temperate | 6×540(720) | 100%-2h | 1,620 | 20,000 | Yes
- Windjammer | Temperate | 5×200 | 500%-24m | 2,500 | 30,000 | Yes
- Barque / Skiff | Tropical | 2×60 / 2×40 | 100% / 200% | 60 / 80 | 100 / 150 | No
- Phoenix / Misty Lady | Flagship | 3×60(120) | 200%-1h | 180 | research | w/ research
- Dying Gull | Flagship | 4(6)×180 | 300%-40m | 1,080 | research | Yes
- Interceptor | Flagship | 6×540 | 600%-20m | 9,720 | research | Yes
- Blue Pearl | Custodian | 6×1000 | 1200%-10m | 36,000 | Pirate custodian | Yes

ROLE PATTERN: each region has a slow high-capacity hauler (100% speed, 2h, big slots) and a fast low-capacity courier (200-1200% speed). Temperate starters (Cog, Caravel) and Tropical starters (Barque, Skiff) are region-LOCKED (No cross). Region-crossing hulls (Hulk and up) gate access to other climate regions. Flagships are unlocked via research; the Blue Pearl is a Custodian/Pirate super-ship. Improved Sails research = ships 10% faster within a region.
systems.research:
CREATIVITY: generated passively by population, DECOUPLED from luxury satisfaction (more inhabitants = more Creativity, diminishing returns), so research never stalls during an economy transition. Exact Creativity-per-resident curve is undocumented.

11 TREES — one per tier (Pioneers...Workers, plus DLC Apprentices/Adepts/Archmages) PLUS an endless INFINITE TREE with its own currency. Each population tier generates its own colour of Creativity (diminishing returns as it grows); higher-tier Creativity in a region can also buy LOWER-tier perks. Once a tier's tree is exhausted, surplus flows into the Infinite Tree (repeatable perks, escalating cost). Region access is itself research (Tropical Islands, Northern Islands). 200+ perks total.

REPRESENTATIVE BASE-TREE PERKS (Perk | Tree | Cost | Effect): Sawmill | Pioneers | 10 | saws double wood output (must be river-placed); Axehammer | Pioneers | 125 | buildings cost 5 less Wood; 'Touch'/tapping perks | various | 5+ | idle auto-tapping of raw tiles (e.g. Sprouting Touch); Optimized Construction | per tier | 150-200 | buildings cost 10 less of a material; Storage Expansion | per tier | 50-125 | +50 to +600 island storage; Garrison Expansion | all | 75-150 | +20 garrison capacity; Optimized Accounting | all | 125-175 | +5% taxes from inhabitants; Slack Time | all | 100-150 | +10% Creativity generation; War Drums | all | 150-200 | battles 10-30 min shorter; Bannerman | all | — | assign more units to a battle; Better Spyglasses | all | 100-150 | island discoveries 35% faster; Improved Sails | all | 150-200 | ships 10% faster within a region; Bribery | — | — | unlocks bribing enemy camps/warchiefs; Marble Pit / Coal Pit | — | — | marble/coal deposits no longer need a mountain; Tropical Islands | Townsmen | 25 | unlock the Tropical region (+1 island); Northern Islands | Merchants | 175 | unlock the Northern region (+1 island).

INFINITE TREE PERKS (repeatable, escalating cost) (Perk | Base cost | Per-rank effect): Bigger Islands | 400 | +2 max island size; Better Relations | 200 | +1 discoverable island slot; Storage Expansion | 150 | +100 storage; Garrison Expansion | 200 | +20 garrison; War Drums | 300 | all battles 20 min shorter; Bannerman | 200 | +5 battle units; Wage Optimization | 250 | -5 coins per building; Optimized Construction | 300 | -2 of each build material; Better Spyglasses | 200 | -35% discovery time.
systems.prestige:
ROGUELITE CORE. SOFT WIN: grow one island to ~1000 satisfied Paragons, build a PALACE (Foundation + 5 stages). Paragon-line Palace is fueled by accumulated FAVOR; the Worker-line Palace has its own track fueled by COINS. Each completed Palace grants one permanent, NON-CONSUMABLE REPUTATION point, spent on CUSTODIANS (run-modifiers, re-pickable each restart). Optionally take one of 12 CHALLENGES (Ironman = no custodians) for bonus Reputation. Selecting a Custodian RESTARTS the world (New Game+).

PALACE STAGE COSTS (Stage | Paragon/Favor | Worker/Coins):
- Foundation: Paragon = 10,000 Coin + 3,000 Plank + 2,000 Tool (requires 500 Paragons) · Worker = 10,000 Coin + 1,000 Fiber + 500 Brick + 1,000 Fine Wood
- Tier 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 (Favor): 1,875 / 3,750 / 7,500 / 15,000
- Tier 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 (Coins): 20,625 / 41,250 / 82,500 / 165,000
- Tier 5: cost 1,000 Fireworks; upgrading consumes ~500 materials/sec

30 CUSTODIANS (★ = Isle of Magicians DLC) by Rep cost:
Rep 1: Bannerman (+50 units to a battle); Cartographer (start with 30 cartography); Chronomancer ★ (ships 2x faster on High Seas); Gatherer (start with all tapping research); Minion (discover 2 more islands at start, don't count to cap); Pillager (pillage research + pillager units, +1% loot); Scientist (start with 50 creativity per population); Scrap Collector (random materials per camp defeated); Sea Trader (+1 ship storage slot).
Rep 2: Arcanologist ★ (start with Isle of Magicians); Jungle Explorer (start with tropical island); Lucky Fellow (starter islands +6 size, +fertility/coal); Merchant (2x trade income & volume); Pirate (start with Blue Pearl super-ship); Quartermaster (garrisons hold 2x units); Warehouseman (island storage doubled).
Rep 3: Enchanter ★ (4x coins/favor on handover, 2x cartography); Inventor (all creativity doubled); Navigator (ships 2x faster within regions & discovering); Negotiator (bribe from start, 2x limit, 1/2 price); Smuggler (ships 2x cargo space).
Rep 4: General (2x battle units, doubles other battle bonuses); Necromancer (50% chance fallen units resurrect); Shapeshifter ★ (bribed enemies join your army); Treasurer (stocked Kontor IV + 10,000 coins at start).
Rep 8: Berserk (all battles finish instantly); Mercenary (base garrison tripled, full garrisons at start); Portal Mage ★ (one home portal per region conjuring goods); Prince (starting islands 2x size with optimal features).
(Note: 30 listed including 5 DLC ★ — Chronomancer, Arcanologist, Enchanter, Shapeshifter, Portal Mage.)

12 CHALLENGES (4 bands — drawback / reward):
- Relaxed: Mobilization (10% of survivors quit after each battle / garrison doubled); Northern Wasteland (no trees on northern islands / all forests always 3 trees); Ship Certificates (ships cost more the more you own / no initial coin cost).
- Tricky: Bulk Cargo (one cargo hold / as large as all combined); Tiny Houses (houses hold only 5 / creativity 2x faster); Unlucky Fellow (size-12 islands, no mountains/rivers / start with 3 cogs + garrison).
- Tough: Massive Construction (materials 4x / coin costs 1/4); Pacifist (cannot attack / start with Bribery, 2,000 coins, unlimited bribes); Reinforcements (attacking grows other camps / units need 1/2 militias to train).
- Impossible: Archipelago (only size-12 islands / +200 island slots); Independence (cannot hand islands to Paragons / +10 island slots); War Weariness (all used units quit after each battle / troops don't count to battle duration).

PROGRESSION/UX: One continuous procedural run (no campaign/scenarios/levels). Advisor Lord Ricardo Southburgh issues real-time text tasks. Unlock gating: default -> population threshold -> research perk. Difficulty: 4 presets + Custom (default = 2nd-hardest); lower levels add an instant battle simulator. Multi-slot saves + backups (since 1.0.69); Steam Cloud, no auto-sync; NO Steam achievements in PP2 (PP1 had 70).
systems.notes: Currency list confirmed verbatim at HTML lines 260 and 573: "Six realm-wide 'Global' currencies — Coin, Cartography, Favor, Reputation, Mana, Elder Mana" (Mana ★ and Elder Mana ★ are Isle of Magicians DLC). Favor formula sourced from line 379. Boss "tier" (100-600) is explicitly a DURATION weight, not HP — do NOT derive health from it. Bula = 2,500 HP (the old "5,000" figure is stale). The 30-Custodian table contains 28 named entries in the doc grid (Rep 1 x9, Rep 2 x7, Rep 3 x5, Rep 4 x4, Rep 8 x4 = 29 shown); the summary header states 30 with 5 DLC ★. Source file: C:/Users/grima/Documents/aiDev/docs/paragon-pioneers-2-clone-research.html (lines 1215-1492 for combat/ships/research/prestige; supporting Favor/turn-in at 377-379, residence Favor at 453, DLC Mana at 456, currency summary at 573).

arch.recommended_architecture:
Deterministic, data-catalog architecture in Godot 4 / GDScript, matching the existing puzzleDrag2/godot house style: `class_name XConfig` const-dict catalogs + stateless static helpers (reachable headless, before the scene tree exists); ONE mutable sim object with `to_dict()`/`from_dict()`; RNG injected explicitly; sim decoupled from rendering via an `AssetRegistry` resolved by string id; and `extends SceneTree` golden-trace tests that exit 0/1.

DATA FLOW (3 layers): Config catalogs (const) → WorldState (mutable) → Golden trace + AssetRegistry outputs. The sim is a pure function of (seed, configs, tick count). Everything is integer arithmetic over storage dictionaries, so the state hash is bit-exact across platforms.

LAYER 1 — Config catalogs (const, immutable): GoodConfig, RecipeConfig, BuildingConfig, PopTierConfig, ServiceConfig, RegionConfig, MapConfig, ShipConfig, MilitaryConfig, ResearchConfig, CustodianConfig, ChallengeConfig. Characteristics: stateless static helpers, defensive copies, `*_IDS` arrays for deterministic iteration order.

LAYER 2 — WorldState (mutable): holds seed · tick · 6 global currencies; research / custodian / challenge selection; and an `IslandState[]` array (each IslandState = terrain · storage · buildings · pop). Method `advance(n)` runs fixed integer ticks. Invariant: `advance(a)+advance(b) == advance(a+b)`. RNG is used ONLY for mapgen / battle.

LAYER 3 — Outputs: (a) Golden trace = (seed, config, N ticks) → SHA-256(hash_state), executed via `run_*_tests.gd` exiting 0/1; (b) AssetRegistry = id → primitive now, sprite later; the sim never references a texture, so zero engine change when art lands.

NOT autoload-heavy: the design centers on const catalogs + one WorldState object rather than many singletons. Reuses existing SaveManager.

PP2-SYSTEM → MODULE/CONFIG MAPPING (verbatim):
- ~166 goods + 6 currencies → GoodConfig; `is_global` flags the 6 currencies → WorldState, rest → per-island storage.
- Production recipes → RecipeConfig; fields: `building, iteration_seconds, inputs, output, qty, terrain, adjacency_bonus`; ONE recipe per building.
- 300+ buildings + unlock gates → BuildingConfig; fields: `tier, kind, unlock{type,tier,count}, cost(incl coin), capacity, terrain_req, footprint(PLACEHOLDER), region`.
- 7 base + 3 DLC tiers + cascade → PopTierConfig; cascade encoded ONCE: `basics_of(tier)==luxuries_of(prev)`, golden-asserted; Hat = terminal flag.
- 14 services (no radius) → ServiceConfig; binary coverage / services-as-goods; NO radius field.
- Shared island stockpile → IslandState.storage; single pool — producers deposit, consumers draw; replaces "supply radius".
- No-roads connectivity → Logistics.recompute_connectivity; placement-time boolean per building, not a per-tick scan; v1 = all-connected.
- Auto-battle vs Orcs → MilitaryConfig + Battle.resolve(army,camp,rng); 3-phase simultaneous strikes, crit ×2, win-if-all-enemies-die; seeded + reproducible.
- Procedural islands → MapConfig + MapGen.generate(params,rng); size/mountains/rivers/fertilities(≤4)/deposits(≤4); seed-stable golden hash.
- Prestige loop → CustodianConfig + ChallengeConfig; 30 custodians (5 DLC), 12 challenges; reputation never consumed.
- Versioned save → SaveManager (reuse); `{version, state}`; discard-on-mismatch; accumulators saved for mid-cycle identity.
- All visuals by id → AssetRegistry; primitive now, sprite later; zero engine change when art lands.
arch.data_model:
GOODS: ~166 goods + 6 currencies, all in GoodConfig. An `is_global` flag distinguishes: the 6 currencies are global → live in WorldState; all other goods → per-island storage (IslandState.storage). The 6 global currencies sit alongside seed and tick in WorldState.

RECIPES: RecipeConfig, ONE recipe per building. Fields verbatim: `building, iteration_seconds, inputs, output, qty, terrain, adjacency_bonus`.

BUILDINGS: 300+ buildings in BuildingConfig with unlock gates. Fields verbatim: `tier, kind, unlock{type,tier,count}, cost(incl coin), capacity, terrain_req, footprint(PLACEHOLDER — no source for tile footprints), region`.

POP TIERS: PopTierConfig — 7 base + 3 DLC tiers + cascade. The cascade is encoded ONCE as the invariant `basics_of(tier) == luxuries_of(prev)`, golden-asserted. Hat is a terminal flag (final tier).

SERVICES: ServiceConfig — 14 services, binary coverage / services-as-goods, NO radius field.

ISLAND STORAGE: IslandState.storage is a single shared pool (stockpile) — producers deposit, consumers draw — replacing any "supply radius" concept. Each IslandState carries: terrain · storage · buildings · pop.

OTHER CATALOGS: RegionConfig, MapConfig, ShipConfig (15-ship roster), MilitaryConfig, ResearchConfig (11 trees + Infinite), CustodianConfig (30 custodians incl. 5 DLC), ChallengeConfig (12 challenges).

All state is integer arithmetic over storage dictionaries → bit-exact state hash across platforms.
arch.tick_model:
TIME BASE: One in-game second = one tick (PP2's denominators are already expressed in seconds). The per-tick resolution order is a const in code and asserted by a golden test.

PER-TICK RESOLUTION ORDER (fixed, golden-asserted; runs in this exact sequence):
1. PRODUCTION — each building advances an integer `progress` counter; on reaching `iteration_seconds`, if inputs are in island storage AND terrain/adjacency satisfied, consume inputs and add output. Stalls hold partial progress (progress is retained, not reset).
2. CONSUMPTION / NEEDS — per house, per resident: integer accumulators draw 1 unit / period_sec from island storage. Services = binary (a supplied provider exists).
3. POPULATION — occupancy = capacity × fraction of basics met, stepped with hysteresis (emigrate only after K unmet ticks). Upgrade-eligible flag set when all needs + luxuries at 100%.
4. ECONOMY / PAYOUT — at 100% luxuries a tier emits its payout (coin/favor → global wallet; militia/slinger → island unit pool). Creativity accrues passively.
5. LOGISTICS — trade routes tick ship/cargo-queue counters, transferring goods/units between island storages (portals = 0-duration edges). Population NEVER moves.

DETERMINISM / IDLE & OFFLINE CATCH-UP: WorldState.advance(n) runs fixed integer ticks with the invariant advance(a)+advance(b) == advance(a+b), which guarantees offline catch-up over a large gap equals the same number of real-time ticks. Same seed → byte-identical tick log. RNG used ONLY for mapgen / battle, so production/consumption are pure integer math. Offline simulation: wall-clock time enters ONLY as a Δt input — the sim advances Δt ticks. Fractional rates with denominators N up to 45,400 seconds over long offline gaps must not drift; large-Δt catch-up correctness is proven in M1. SaveManager persists per-resident/per-building integer accumulators so a mid-cycle save resumes with bit-identical partial progress (mid-cycle identity).
milestones: M0 — Spec, schema & harness: system inventory + non-features; dev correction; catalog skeletons with `fidelity` + `open_question` fields. Gate: schema loads headless, no dangling ids, cascade expressible. Risk: low.
M1 — Deterministic sim core + grid: square grid; terrain & placement constraints; fixed tick + seeded RNG. Gate: same seed → byte-identical tick log; placement legality. Risk: med.
M2 — Range logistics + Island Storage: warehouse-pool model; services-as-goods; adjacency bonuses. Gate: in-range produces / out stalls; storage conservation. Risk: med.

M3 — Population & luxury cascade: 2 tracks, capacities, basic/luxury baskets, the cascade, emigration, payout-at-100%. Gate: cascade invariant numeric; occupancy up / emigrate; upgrade gated. Risk: HIGH.
M4 — Production chains at scale: 300+ buildings/recipes; pop/research unlock gates; 4-caps. Gate: golden chain throughput; thresholds fire; loop closes to a need. Risk: med.
M5 — Coin economy & Kontor: Coin wallet sink/source; Kontor I–V; Cargo Kontor. Gate: debit on build / credit on payout; never negative. Risk: med.
M6 — Ships, trade routes & regions: regions-of-islands; 15-ship roster; Simple/Complex routes; climate gating. Gate: routes ferry goods/units; population rejected; region-lock enforced. Risk: med.
M7 — Procedural islands & Cartography: map generator; +10 min/carto point; discovery loop. Gate: same seed+params → identical island (golden hash); 4-caps. Risk: HIGH.
M8 — Auto-resolved Orc combat: 3-phase battle; abilities; duration formula; pop→units→training; 12 bosses. Gate: same army vs camp → identical damage log; win-on-mutual-death. Risk: med.
M9 — Research / Creativity: passive Creativity; 11 trees + Infinite; region unlocks; gating perks. Gate: creativity accrues; region perk → +island; perk ungates a building. Risk: med.

M10 — Palace / Reputation / Custodian prestige: 1000-Paragon soft win; palace 5 stages; 30 custodians; 12 challenges; restart. Gate: palace → 1 reputation; custodian persists+applies next run; deterministic restart. Risk: med-hi.
M11 — Full-content fidelity & balance parity: complete catalogs; resolve/freeze open numbers; optional DLC scaffold. Gate: scope counts; balance goldens; no unfrozen unknowns. Risk: med.

M12+ — Divergence (build on top): indirect-control logistics, explainability overlay, optional events behind faithful-mode flag; pull reusable systems up into godot-core. Gate: per-feature gate; must not regress any M0–M11 golden. Risk: scope.
arch.risks:
Six fidelity risks (prototype-first), each tagged to a milestone with a de-risk strategy:

1. Procedural island determinism (M7) [danger]: If the seeded generator isn't byte-identical across runs/platforms, every downstream golden trace is non-reproducible. De-risk FIRST: standalone seeded generator + golden-island-hash test before any discovery wiring.

2. The luxury cascade & exact consumption (M3) [danger]: The cascade coupling + 55 denominators define all pacing; the treadmill is the signature tension. Denominators are verified — transcribe exactly. De-risk: prototype one Pioneer→Colonist loop asserting same-good-same-rate before scaling.

3. Range / service radii (M2/M3): No public radius numbers exist — and the real model is warehouse-pool + services-as-goods, NOT a circle. De-risk: make connectivity a single tunable; test the mechanism, not tile counts; freeze in M11.

4. Battle phase resolution (M8): Simultaneity, Double-in-both-phases, kill-ordering, Flanking/Trample, win-on-mutual-death are easy to get subtly wrong. De-risk: prototype the resolver against the data-mined stat lines; lock a golden damage log.

5. Idle fixed-tick correctness (M1): Fractional rates (N up to 45,400s) over long offline gaps must not drift. De-risk: prove determinism + large-Δt catch-up in M1; wall-clock enters only as a Δt input.

6. Unresolvable numbers (M5/M11) [teal]: gold/sec, Kontor prices, island-Coin formula, footprints, boss-tier discrepancy have no source. De-risk: pick documented-plausible defaults, FREEZE as canon, log each in the open-questions ledger. The Favor formula IS published — use it verbatim.
arch.open_questions:
Open-questions ledger — numbers a faithful balance pass still needs. Each must be measured in-game, data-mined from the Unity build (AssetRipper/UABEA on the Android APK `com.GniGames.ParagonPioneers2`), or frozen as a chosen default with a ledger note.

1. Per-building TILE FOOTPRINTS — Status: no source. Close: decompile building prefabs/ScriptableObjects for a `gridSize`/`Vector2Int`, or measure from build-mode screenshots. Expect 1×1–3×3.

2. SIZE→TILE-COUNT mapping & pixels-per-tile — Status: no source. Close: measure at Sizes 10/20/34; PP1 hint: a 13×11 block suits "island 14+", so Size ≈ buildable edge length.

3. Per-resident COIN OUTPUT rate — Status: no source. Close: model `base_rate × luxury_fraction`; PP1 shape ~5–6× per tier; datamine for exact.

4. SUPPLY/SERVICE RANGES & warehouse throttle — Status: model known. Close: real model is warehouse-pool + services-as-goods (no radius); ~1 building/30s is a PP1 dev figure — tune.

5. ISLE OF MAGICIANS recipe ratios, needs, capacities, Mana rates — Status: no source. Close: decompile DLC asset bundle (app 3059310); building roster + Mana/Elder-Mana split are known, ratios aren't.

6. KONTOR buy/sell prices & trade volume — Status: no source. Close: decompile trade-offer tables; freeze defaults otherwise.

7. BOSS TIER values (100/150/200/300 vs 200/300/400/600) — Status: resolved-ish. Close: tiers are duration weights, not HP — HP table is authoritative; treat tier as a tunable.

8. Difficulty-preset numeric effects; advisor task list; save-slot count — Status: partial. Close: datamine config; reconstruct the scripted advisor queue from a playthrough.
